en: Now I would remind you, brothers, of the gospel I preached to you, which you received, in which you stand,
zh: 弟兄們，我要把我從前傳給你們的福音向你們講明。這福音你們已經領受了，並且靠著它站立得穩。

en: and by which you are being saved, if you hold fast to the word I preached to you- unless you believed in vain.
zh: 你們若持守我所傳給你們的道，就必靠這福音得救，不然就是徒然相信了。

en: For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the Scriptures,
zh: 我從前領受了又傳交給你們那最要緊的，就是基督照著聖經所記的，為我們的罪死了，

en: that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the Scriptures,
zh: 又埋葬了，又照著聖經所記的，第三天復活了；

en: and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
zh: 並且曾經向磯法顯現，然後向十二使徒顯現。

en: Then he appeared to more than five hundred brothers at one time, most of whom are still alive, though some have fallen asleep.
zh: 以後又有一次向五百多個弟兄顯現。他們中間大多數到現今還在，也有些已經睡了。

en: Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles.
zh: 以後也向雅各顯現，再後又向眾使徒顯現，

en: Last of all, as to one untimely born, he appeared also to me.
zh: 最後也向我顯現；我好像一個未到產期而生的人。

en: For I am the least of the apostles, unworthy to be called an apostle, because I persecuted the church of God.
zh: 我原是使徒中最小的，本來沒有資格稱為使徒，因為我曾經迫害 神的教會。

en: But by the grace of God I am what I am, and his grace toward me was not in vain. On the contrary, I worked harder than any of them, though it was not I, but the grace of God that is with me.
zh: 然而靠著 神的恩典，我得以有了今天，而且他賜給我的恩典並沒有落空；我比眾使徒格外勞苦，其實不是我，而是 神的恩典與我同在。

en: Whether then it was I or they, so we preach and so you believed.
zh: 因此，不論是我，或是眾使徒，我們這樣傳，你們也這樣信了。

en: Now if Christ is proclaimed as raised from the dead, how can some of you say that there is no resurrection of the dead?
zh: 我們既然傳基督從死人中復活了，你們中間怎麼有人說沒有死人復活呢？

en: But if there is no resurrection of the dead, then not even Christ has been raised.
zh: 倘若沒有死人復活的事，基督也就沒有復活了。

en: And if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is in vain and your faith is in vain.
zh: 如果基督沒有復活，我們所傳的就是枉然，你們的信也是枉然，

en: We are even found to be misrepresenting God, because we testified about God that he raised Christ, whom he did not raise if it is true that the dead are not raised.
zh: 我們也會被人認為是替 神作假見證的了，因為我們為 神作過見證說，他使基督復活了。如果死人沒有復活， 神也就沒有使基督復活了。

en: For if the dead are not raised, not even Christ has been raised.
zh: 因為如果死人沒有復活，基督也就沒有復活。

en: And if Christ has not been raised, your faith is futile and you are still in your sins.
zh: 基督若沒有復活，你們的信就是徒然，你們仍在你們的罪裡。

en: Then those also who have fallen asleep in Christ have perished.
zh: 那麼，在基督裡睡了的人也就滅亡了。

en: If in this life only we have hoped in Christ, we are of all people most to be pitied.
zh: 如果我們在基督裡只在今生有盼望，就比所有人更可憐了。

en: But in fact Christ has been raised from the dead, the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.
zh: 現在基督已經從死人中復活，成為睡了的人初熟的果子。

en: For as by a man came death, by a man has come also the resurrection of the dead.
zh: 死既藉著一人而來，死人復活也藉著一人而來。

en: For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ shall all be made alive.
zh: 在亞當裡眾人都死了，照樣，在基督裡眾人也都要復活；

en: But each in his own order: Christ the firstfruits, then at his coming those who belong to Christ.
zh: 只是各人要按著自己的次序：初熟的果子是基督，以後，在他來的時候，是那些屬基督的人。

en: Then comes the end, when he delivers the kingdom to God the Father after destroying every rule and every authority and power.
zh: 再後，末期到了的時候，基督把所有的統治者、掌權者和有能者都毀滅了，就把國度交給父 神。

en: For he must reign until he has put all his enemies under his feet.
zh: 因為基督必要作王，直到 神把所有的仇敵都放在他的腳下。

en: The last enemy to be destroyed is death.
zh: 最後要毀滅的仇敵就是死，

en: For "God has put all things in subjection under his feet." But when it says, "all things are put in subjection," it is plain that he is excepted who put all things in subjection under him.
zh: 因為經上記著說：“他使萬物都服在他腳下。”既然說萬物都服了他，顯然那使萬物服他的就不包括在內了。

en: When all things are subjected to him, then the Son himself will also be subjected to him who put all things in subjection under him, that God may be all in all.
zh: 等到萬物都服了他，那時子自己也要服那位叫萬物服他的，好使 神在萬有之上。

en: Otherwise, what do people mean by being baptized on behalf of the dead? If the dead are not raised at all, why are people baptized on their behalf?
zh: 不然，那些為死人受洗的，是為了甚麼呢？如果死人根本不會復活，那麼為甚麼要為他們受洗呢？

en: Why am I in danger every hour?
zh: 我們又為甚麼時刻冒險呢？

en: I protest, brothers, by my pride in you, which I have in Christ Jesus our Lord, I die every day!
zh: 弟兄們，我是天天冒死的，這是我憑著我在我們的主耶穌基督裡，因你們而誇口說的。

en: What do I gain if, humanly speaking, I fought with beasts at Ephesus? If the dead are not raised, "Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die."
zh: 我在以弗所和野獸搏鬥，如果照著人的意思來看，那對我有甚麼益處呢？如果死人不會復活， “我們就吃吃喝喝吧， 因為我們明天就要死了。”

en: Do not be deceived: "Bad company ruins good morals."
zh: 你們不要自欺， “濫交朋友是會敗壞品德的。”

en: Wake up from your drunken stupor, as is right, and do not go on sinning. For some have no knowledge of God. I say this to your shame.
zh: 你們理當醒悟過來，不要犯罪，因為你們中間有人不認識 神；我說這話是要你們羞愧。

en: But someone will ask, "How are the dead raised? With what kind of body do they come?"
zh: 但有人會說：“死人怎樣復活呢？要帶著怎麼樣的身體來呢？”

en: You foolish person! What you sow does not come to life unless it dies.
zh: 無知的人哪，你所種的若不死去，就不能生。

en: And what you sow is not the body that is to be, but a bare kernel, perhaps of wheat or of some other grain.
zh: 你們所種的，不是那將來要長成的形體，只不過是一粒種子，也許是麥子或別的種子。

en: But God gives it a body as he has chosen, and to each kind of seed its own body.
zh: 但 神隨著自己的意思給它一個形體，給每一樣種子各有自己的形體。

en: For not all flesh is the same, but there is one kind for humans, another for animals, another for birds, and another for fish.
zh: 而且各種身體也都不一樣，人有人的身體，獸有獸的身體，鳥有鳥的身體，魚有魚的身體。

en: There are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies, but the glory of the heavenly is of one kind, and the glory of the earthly is of another.
zh: 有天上的形體，也有地上的形體；天上形體的榮光是一樣，地上形體的榮光又是一樣。

en: There is one glory of the sun, and another glory of the moon, and another glory of the stars; for star differs from star in glory.
zh: 太陽有太陽的榮光，月亮有月亮的榮光，星星有星星的榮光，而且每一顆星的榮光也都不同。

en: So is it with the resurrection of the dead. What is sown is perishable; what is raised is imperishable.
zh: 死人復活也是這樣。所種的是必朽壞的，復活的是不朽壞的；

en: It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.
zh: 所種的是卑賤的，復活的是榮耀的；所種的是軟弱的，復活的是有能力的；

en: It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body, there is also a spiritual body.
zh: 所種的是屬血氣的身體，復活的是屬靈的身體。既然有屬血氣的身體，也會有屬靈的身體。

en: Thus it is written, "The first man Adam became a living being"; the last Adam became a life-giving spirit.
zh: 經上也是這樣記著說：“第一個人亞當成了有生命的活人，末後的亞當成了使人活的靈。”

en: But it is not the spiritual that is first but the natural, and then the spiritual.
zh: 但那首先的不是屬靈的，而是屬血氣的，然後才有屬靈的。

en: The first man was from the earth, a man of dust; the second man is from heaven.
zh: 第一個人是出於地，是屬土的；第二個人是出於天。

en: As was the man of dust, so also are those who are of the dust, and as is the man of heaven, so also are those who are of heaven.
zh: 那屬土的怎樣，所有屬土的也都怎樣；屬天的怎樣，所有屬天的也都怎樣。

en: Just as we have borne the image of the man of dust, we shall also bear the image of the man of heaven.
zh: 我們既然有了屬土的形象，將來也必有屬天的形象。

en: I tell you this, brothers: flesh and blood cannot inherit the kingdom of God, nor does the perishable inherit the imperishable.
zh: 弟兄們，我告訴你們，血肉之體不能承受 神的國，必朽壞的也不能承受那不朽壞的。

en: Behold! I tell you a mystery. We shall not all sleep, but we shall all be changed,
zh: 我現在把一個奧祕告訴你們：我們不是都要睡覺，而是在一剎那，眨眼之間，就是號角最後一次吹響的時候，我們都要改變；因為號角要吹響，死人要復活，成為不朽壞的，我們也要改變。

en: in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye, at the last trumpet. For the trumpet will sound, and the dead will be raised imperishable, and we shall be changed.
For this perishable body must put on the imperishable, and this mortal body must put on immortality.
zh: 這必朽壞的必須穿上不朽壞的，這必死的必須穿上不死的；

en: When the perishable puts on the imperishable, and the mortal puts on immortality, then shall come to pass the saying that is written: "Death is swallowed up in victory."
zh: 這必朽壞的既穿上了不朽壞的，這必死的既穿上了不死的，那時，經上的話就應驗了： “勝利了！死亡已經被吞滅。

en: "O death, where is your victory? O death, where is your sting?"
zh: 死亡啊！你的勝利在哪裡？ 死亡啊！你的毒刺在哪裡？”

en: The sting of death is sin, and the power of sin is the law.
zh: 死的毒刺就是罪，罪的權勢就是律法。

en: But thanks be to God, who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ.
zh: 感謝 神，他藉著我們的主耶穌基督，把勝利賜給我們。

en: Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.
zh: 所以，我親愛的弟兄們，你們務要堅固，不可動搖，常常竭力多作主工，因為知道你們的勞苦，在主裡面不是徒然的。
